task_name: add Mistral Instruct v3 chat template

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here is the complete plan. **Only `common/chat.cpp` is touched** — no headers, no existing handlers.

---

## What will be added

### 1. Handler function — inserted before `namespace workaround` (~line 1518)

`common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3()` follows the exact same skeleton as every other specialized handler:

| Field | Value / behaviour |
|---|---|
| `prompt` | `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl()` — Jinja renders `[INST]…[/INST]` and system-prepend natively |
| `preserved_tokens` | `"[INST]"`, `"[/INST]"`, `"[TOOL_CALLS]"` |
| No tools / `NONE` | `p.content(p.rest())` — plain text response |
| With tools | `p.standard_json_tools("[TOOL_CALLS]", "", ..., array_wrapped=true, name="name", args="arguments", call_id="id")` — matches the Nemo format: `[TOOL_CALLS][{"name":"fn","arguments":{…},"id":"abc123def"}]` |
| Grammar trigger | `COMMON_GRAMMAR_TRIGGER_TYPE_WORD` on `"[TOOL_CALLS]"` (lazy for AUTO) |
| Thinking | None (`supports_thinking` stays `false`) |

### 2. Detection block — inserted in `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` right after the Ministral-3 block (~line 1644)

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: mistral-instruct-v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The two conditions are **mutually exclusive** with the existing Ministral-3 check (which *requires* `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`), so ordering is safe.

details.reasoning: Implementation successfully adds the mistral-instruct-v3 handler with almost all requirements met: new static function created, proper auto-detection logic with [SYSTEM_PROMPT] absence check, LOG_DBG call present, handler dispatched correctly with (tmpl, params), and no modifications to existing handlers. However, the format is set to COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE instead of the required COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY, which is an explicit requirement violation. The rest of the implementation is comprehensive and well-structured, including tool call support and grammar handling.
